sections.name: listening
sections.description: Part 1 (15 items): Listen to dialogues and judge whether statements are true or false. Part 2 (15 items): Listen to interviews/conversations and answer multiple-choice questions. Part 3 (15 items): Listen to extended monologues, news, or academic lectures and answer comprehension questions.

sections.name: reading
sections.description: Part 1 (15 items): Choose the correct word or phrase to fill blanks in complex sentences. Part 2 (15 items): Read medium passages and answer multiple-choice comprehension questions. Part 3 (15 items): Read longer, more complex texts (news articles, academic excerpts) and answer comprehension questions requiring inference and analysis.

sections.name: writing
sections.description: Part 1 (1 item): Read a passage of approximately 1000 characters, then write an 400-character summary capturing the main points. Part 2 (1 item): Write a composition of at least 80 characters based on a picture prompt, expressing your view or telling a story.

scoring_notes: Each section scored 0–100. Total is sum of three sections. 180/300 required to pass. Writing section includes both a summary task and a composition. HSK 5 certifies advanced proficiency equivalent to C1 on the CEFR.
